title: Formatting Hyperlinks in Gmail Automation
excerpt:
I am using a Monday.com board to maintain my client data, and one of the columns contains a link field. For example, I have a Google Forms link like this: 
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfxu_3GGsw7cU0jDy7AOBAD…

posts.author: Timothy7 (Timothy Kariuki | CreativeBits LLC)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am using a Monday.com board to maintain my client data, and one of the columns contains a link field. For example, I have a Google Forms link like this:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfxu_3GGsw7cU0jDy7AOBADVQbpSw9DjMq7Qcv49vrhVw/viewform?usp=pp_url&entry.1729430304

With a display text of “intake form link.”

When sending emails through Monday.com’s Gmail automation, the email currently includes the full URL instead of displaying the hyperlink as formatted text. This makes the email look cluttered with long, unformatted URLs.

Is there a way to format hyperlinks in Gmail automation so that only the display text appears as a clickable link? Ideally, I would like to send the email with the hyperlink displayed as:

intake form link (clickable) instead of showing the full URL.

Thank you for your assistance!

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Timothy, Yes! Monday.com’s Gmail automation currently does not automatically format links as clickable text when pulling them from a Link column. However, can you try and format your email using HTML to display a hyperlink with custom text?

Solution: Use HTML in Your Email Body

Instead of inserting the raw {Link Column} value, format it as an HTML link in your automation setup.

Steps to Format the Hyperlink in Gmail Automation:

  1. Go to Automations in your Monday.com board.
  2. Choose or create an automation for sending an email via Gmail.
  3. In the email body, use the following format:

html

CopyEdit

<a href="{Link Column}">intake form link</a>
  1. This will render as a clickable hyperlink with the text “intake form link.”
    Let me know with a like if it worked for you!

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:

posts.author: MarigoldsInMay (Darielle Shandler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Clare! I’ve successfully used the html coding to add a hyperlink into a Form question description, however when I used it in an email automation, it didn’t work. See image below to how the email came in for me. Do you know of another solution?

Screenshot 2025-03-27 at 5.49.24 PM527×168 14.1 KB

posts.author: charlieg (Charlie Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml:
HTML formatting in email automations only works if you have the Monday CRM product. Monday is greedy and did not release this very useful feature to Monday Work Management users.

To send an HTML email, you’ll need to either find an app in the Monday app store (I have not found a good one I like yet) or use a third-party integration like Make.com or Zapier. Sorry to be the bearer of bad news!

posts.author: cmak (Clara Mak)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have the Monday CRM product, but the HTML formatting in Gmail automation’s outcome looks exactly like Darielle’s above post.

Now granted, I didn’t use a link column. I just replaced “{Link Column}” with a static URL.

Any solution?

posts.author: charlieg (Charlie Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t have the CRM product, but I have read the documentation. It sound like it is not working properly for you. I would suggest contacting Monday Support. Best of luck!

posts.author: Timothy7 (Timothy Kariuki | CreativeBits LLC)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes we did a research on Supermail but the client was specific on not using a Monday.com third party application so make.com was our next pick
